_meta.note: Demo-safe medical report schema aligned with the Hoh Law intake brief and app validation fields.

patient_name.description: The full name of the patient who received medical services or examination. Found in the patient information section, typically near the top of the report. May be labeled as 'Patient', 'Patient Name', 'Name', 'Client Name', or appear in a demographic information block.

diagnosis.description: The primary medical finding, condition, or diagnosis documented in the report. This is the main clinical conclusion. Found in the body of the report, often in a section labeled 'Diagnosis', 'Impression', 'Clinical Diagnosis', 'Assessment', or 'Conclusion'.

doctor_name.description: The name of the doctor, physician, specialist, or medical professional who authored, signed, or issued the report. Usually appears near the signature block, letterhead, or report footer.

facility_name.description: The hospital, clinic, imaging center, or healthcare facility that issued the report. May appear in the letterhead, logo, address block, or report footer.

report_date.description: The date of the medical report, examination, consultation, discharge, scan, or certificate. May be labeled as 'Date', 'Report Date', 'Visit Date', 'Exam Date', 'Discharge Date', or appear near the report header.

type_of_injury.description: The broad body area affected by the injury. Use 'leg' for knee, foot, ankle, thigh, hip, or lower-limb injuries. Use 'hand' for hand, wrist, arm, finger, or upper-limb injuries. Use 'others' only when the report does not fit these categories.
extend:descriptions: Lower-limb injury such as knee, foot, ankle, thigh, or hip.
Upper-limb injury such as hand, wrist, arm, or finger.
Any other injury location or unclear body region.

summary_findings.description: A concise list of the main clinical findings, impressions, diagnoses, restrictions, certificates, or treatment outcomes in the report. Include the most important facts a claims reviewer would need.

region.description: The anatomical region or body part being discussed, such as knee, wrist, ankle, shoulder, spine, ligament, bone, tendon, or soft tissue.

finding.description: The specific clinical finding for that region, including fractures, tears, swelling, tenderness, reduced range of motion, degeneration, imaging results, or normal findings.

is_serious.description: True when the finding suggests significant injury, fracture, tear, surgery, disability, long medical leave, permanent impairment, or escalation-worthy clinical concern.

anatomical_findings.description: Structured findings for specific anatomical regions described in the report. Each item represents a body part or structure and the relevant clinical observation.

medical_leave_days.description: The number of medical leave days, hospitalization leave days, or certified rest days stated in the report or medical certificate. Use null if no duration is stated.

recommendations.description: Recommended follow-up actions, treatment plans, referrals, physiotherapy, medication, specialist review, surgery, rest, or monitoring instructions mentioned in the report.
